proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a different mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a different mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_docetaxel.

Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_olaparib (PARP inhibitor benefit, particularly in HRD/BRCA carriers who are over-represented among olaparib recipients).
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.

analyses.result_summary: Enzalutamide vs no-enzalutamide: 3.728 vs 3.755 months (Welch t-test).
Olaparib vs no-olaparib: 3.829 vs 3.735 months — significant +0.094 month advantage on olaparib (likely driven by BRCA2-mutated subgroup; tested in iter 2).
Pembrolizumab vs not: 3.805 vs 3.741 months — non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between brca2_mutation and treatment_olaparib on pfs_months: BRCA2-mutated patients receive a much larger PFS benefit from olaparib than BRCA2-wildtype patients (PARP synthetic-lethality hypothesis).
There is a positive interaction between psma_high and treatment_lu177_psma on pfs_months: PSMA-high patients receive a larger PFS benefit from Lu177-PSMA radioligand therapy than PSMA-low patients.
There is a positive interaction between msi_high and treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months: MSI-high patients benefit more from pembrolizumab (immune checkpoint inhibitor) than MSI-stable patients.
There is a negative interaction between ar_v7_positive and treatment_enzalutamide on pfs_months: AR-V7-positive patients have reduced enzalutamide benefit (resistance to AR-targeted therapy).
There is a negative interaction between ar_v7_positive and treatment_abiraterone on pfs_months: AR-V7-positive patients have reduced abiraterone benefit (resistance to AR-targeted therapy).

analyses.result_summary: Strong positive interaction. Cell means: BRCA2+/olap+ 5.22 (n=529), BRCA2+/olap- 3.67 (n=4467), BRCA2-/olap+ 3.67 (n=4569), BRCA2-/olap- 3.74 (n=40435). Interaction coef +1.619 months.
No interaction. Cell means within ~0.02 months of each other. Lu177-PSMA shows no enhanced benefit in PSMA-high.
No significant interaction; point estimate is opposite of expected direction (MSI-high/pembro 3.53 vs MSI-high/no-pembro 3.79).

proposed_hypotheses.text: mcrpc patients (castration-resistant disease) have a shorter mean pfs_months than non-mcrpc patients.
Patients with visceral_mets have a shorter mean pfs_months than those without.
Patients with liver_mets have a shorter mean pfs_months than those without.
Patients with bone_mets have a shorter mean pfs_months than those without.
Higher gleason_score is correlated with shorter pfs_months (negative Pearson correlation).
Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher psa_ng_ml is correlated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).

analyses.result_summary: mCRPC patients have substantially shorter PFS: 3.51 vs 4.03 months, diff -0.52 months.
Visceral mets vs not: 3.71 vs 3.75 months — small but significant.
Liver mets vs not: 3.74 vs 3.74 months — no effect.
Bone mets vs not: 3.75 vs 3.74 months — no effect (counter to clinical expectation).
No correlation (r=-0.003) — Gleason score does not predict PFS in this cohort.
Strong gradient: ECOG 0 = 4.68, ECOG 1 = 3.49, ECOG 2 = 2.38 months. Decline of ~1.15 months per grade.
Highly significant negative correlation; log1p(psa) yields r=-0.231.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is positively correlated with pfs_months (better nutritional/hepatic reserve → longer PFS).
Higher ldh_u_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months (tumor burden marker).
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is negatively correlated with pfs_months (bone-disease/liver burden).
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is positively correlated with pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l (inflammation) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
Higher nlr is negatively correlated with pfs_months (systemic inflammation).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is negatively correlated with pfs_months (cachexia).
Higher cea_ng_ml is negatively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Direction is negative (opposite of hypothesis) and barely significant.
Strong negative prognostic correlation — cachexia predicts shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher fatigue_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher pain_nrs is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher dyspnea_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher cough_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher appetite_loss_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: No correlation between fatigue grade and PFS.
No correlation between pain NRS and PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity strata.
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type strata.
rural_residence patients have a different mean pfs_months than non-rural patients.
Higher education_years is correlated with longer pfs_months (proxy for SES/access).
Higher smoking_pack_years is correlated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher age_years is correlated with pfs_months (direction unclear a priori — older patients may have shorter or longer PFS).

analyses.result_summary: No difference: white 3.74, black 3.74, hispanic 3.74, asian 3.80, other 3.75 months.
No difference: medicare 3.75, private 3.74, medicaid 3.74, uninsured 3.73 months.
Extremely strong positive correlation (r=+0.86) — older patients have markedly longer PFS, opposite of typical expectation. Investigated further in iter 7.

proposed_hypotheses.text: pfs_months increases approximately linearly with age_years across the full range of the cohort (refining the iter-6 finding).

analyses.code: Age decile means + smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Monotone increase across deciles: age ~47 → PFS 0.85; age ~82 → PFS 6.86. Linear fit: pfs = -7.57 + 0.174 * age (each year adds ~0.17 months of PFS). Direction is opposite to typical clinical experience.

proposed_hypotheses.text: diabetes_mellitus is associated with shorter pfs_months.
hypertension is associated with shorter pfs_months.
copd is associated with shorter pfs_months.
chronic_kidney_disease is associated with shorter pfs_months.
heart_failure is associated with shorter pfs_months.
depression_anxiety_diagnosis is associated with shorter pfs_months.
prior_malignancy is associated with shorter pfs_months.
hiv_positive is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Trend toward shorter PFS but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: prior_chemotherapy is associated with shorter pfs_months (sicker / heavily pretreated population).
prior_radiation is associated with different pfs_months.
Higher prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Longer years_since_diagnosis is associated with longer pfs_months (selection effect for indolent disease).

At least one of the 25 germline SNP genotypes is associated with pfs_months at conventional p<0.05; the most likely candidates are pharmacogenomic variants in CYP enzymes (snp_rs1065852=CYP2D6, snp_rs4244285=CYP2C19, snp_rs4986893=CYP2C19, snp_rs1799853=CYP2C9).

analyses.code: for snp in snp_cols: stats.pearsonr(df[snp], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Of 25 SNPs, only snp_rs4986893 reaches nominal p<0.05 (r=-0.011, p=0.014); not robust to multiple-testing correction (Bonferroni threshold 0.002). No SNP shows a clinically meaningful effect; mean PFS differences are <0.1 months between genotype groups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: tp53_mutation is associated with shorter pfs_months.
pten_loss is associated with shorter pfs_months (PTEN loss is a known adverse prognostic in prostate cancer).
cdkn2a_loss is associated with shorter pfs_months.

None of the off-target oncogenic alterations (her2_amplification, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, braf_v600e, ntrk_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration, keap1_mutation, pik3ca_mutation) — most of which are not characteristic of prostate cancer — meaningfully affect pfs_months in this cohort.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests across her2_amplification, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, braf_v600e, ntrk_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration, keap1_mutation, pik3ca_mutation
analyses.result_summary: All non-significant (smallest p=0.16 for ntrk_fusion). No meaningful effect of any off-target alteration.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable linear model, age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, ldh_u_l, visceral_mets, brca2_mutation, and treatment_olaparib are each independently associated with pfs_months in their clinically expected directions.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + mcrpc + psa_ng_ml + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + ldh_u_l + alkaline_phosphatase_u_l + hemoglobin_g_dl + visceral_mets + brca2_mutation + treatment_olaparib', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted R²=0.9475. Significant independent effects (per unit): age +0.174, ecog -1.152, mcrpc -0.375, psa -0.0027, albumin +0.478, weight_loss -0.077, ldh -0.0005, visceral_mets -0.021, brca2 +0.155, olaparib +0.146. Hemoglobin and alkaline phosphatase fall to non-significance after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The brca2_mutation x treatment_olaparib interaction on pfs_months remains large and significant after adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, ldh_u_l, and visceral_mets.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +1.437 months (p effectively 0). After adjustment, brca2 main effect (+0.003, p=0.68) and olaparib main effect (-0.003, p=0.72) collapse to ~0 — the entire benefit lives in the BRCA2-positive olaparib-treated cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Number of concurrent treatments (n_treatments) is associated with pfs_months.
Receiving both treatment_enzalutamide and treatment_abiraterone (a clinically unusual combination) is associated with different pfs_months than not receiving the combination.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ n_treatments + age_years + ecog_ps + albumin_g_dl + mcrpc', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment, +0.011 months PFS per additional treatment (p=2e-4). Suggests modestly more treatment ≈ slightly longer PFS.
Enza+abi together vs not: 3.740 vs 3.745 months — no effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age, ECOG, mCRPC, PSA, albumin, weight loss, LDH, and visceral mets, the msi_high x treatment_pembrolizumab interaction on pfs_months is still not significant.
After adjustment, the psma_high x treatment_lu177_psma interaction is still not significant.
After adjustment, the ar_v7_positive x treatment_enzalutamide interaction is still not significant.
After adjustment, the ar_v7_positive x treatment_abiraterone interaction is still not significant.
tp53_mutation x treatment_docetaxel interaction on pfs_months is non-significant after adjustment.
pten_loss x treatment_enzalutamide interaction on pfs_months is non-significant after adjustment.
The brca2_mutation x treatment_olaparib interaction on pfs_months is present in both mcrpc=0 and mcrpc=1 subgroups (i.e., not modified by castration-resistance).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ brca2_mutation*treatment_olaparib', data=sub).fit() within mcrpc strata
analyses.result_summary: mcrpc=0: interaction +1.627 (p=1.3e-28); mcrpc=1: interaction +1.630 (p=6.0e-36). Effect virtually identical in both strata — not modified by castration-resistance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no ECOG x treatment_docetaxel interaction on pfs_months (no differential docetaxel benefit by performance status).
There is no ECOG x treatment_olaparib interaction on pfs_months.
There is no ECOG x treatment_pembrolizumab interaction on pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + albumin_g_dl + mcrpc + ecog_ps*treatment_docetaxel', data=df).fit()
smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + albumin_g_dl + mcrpc + ecog_ps*treatment_olaparib', data=df).fit()
smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + albumin_g_dl + mcrpc + ecog_ps*treatment_pembrolizumab', data=df).fit()

proposed_hypotheses.text: PSA tertile (low/mid/high) is monotonically associated with shorter pfs_months (high tertile shortest).

analyses.result_summary: Low PSA tertile: 4.24 months; mid: 3.77; high: 3.22 months. Clear monotone gradient.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a synergistic (positive) interaction between albumin_g_dl and weight_loss_pct_6mo on pfs_months — i.e. low-albumin/high-weight-loss patients have disproportionately worse PFS than additive effects suggest.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ albumin_g_dl*weight_loss_pct_6mo + age_years + ecog_ps + mcrpc', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.001, p=0.38. No multiplicative interaction; the two prognostic markers act additively on PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The brca2_mutation x treatment_olaparib interaction on pfs_months is present in all major race_ethnicity strata (white, black, hispanic, asian, other) with similar effect sizes.
The brca2_mutation x treatment_olaparib interaction on pfs_months is present across all insurance_type strata (medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).
The brca2_mutation x treatment_olaparib interaction on pfs_months is present across age tertiles.

analyses.result_summary: white +1.71 (p=9e-46), black +1.67 (p=1e-8), asian +1.57 (p=1e-4), hispanic +1.00 (p=9e-5), other +3.57 (p=5e-6). All significant; magnitudes broadly similar across strata.
medicare +1.49 (p=5e-23), private +1.69 (p=2e-26), medicaid +1.95 (p=3e-14), uninsured +1.16 (p=0.013). Significant across all strata.
young +1.26, mid +1.48, old +1.57 — all p<1e-32. Effect mildly increases with age but is robust everywhere.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no clinically meaningful age x treatment interaction on pfs_months for any of the primary therapies (enzalutamide, abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ ecog_ps + albumin_g_dl + mcrpc + age_years*<tx>', data=df).fit() for each tx
analyses.result_summary: All age x treatment interaction coefficients are within ±0.001 month/year and non-significant: enza p=0.54, abi p=0.27, doc p=0.73, olap p=0.70.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for clinical and biomarker covariates, only treatment_olaparib (with its dependence on brca2_mutation) shows an independent main-effect benefit on pfs_months; the AR-targeted, taxane, radioligand, and immune-checkpoint therapies show no independent benefit.

analyses.result_summary: enza -0.005 p=0.26, abi -0.0001 p=0.97, doc -0.007 p=0.14, olap +0.146 p=3e-100, lu177-psma -0.007 p=0.26, pembro +0.017 p=0.08. Only olaparib reaches strong significance; pembrolizumab marginal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within BRCA2-mutated patients, treatment_olaparib produces a large positive PFS benefit; within BRCA2-wildtype patients, treatment_olaparib produces no benefit (and possibly harm).

analyses.code: Welch t-test treatment_olaparib stratified by brca2_mutation
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+: olap_on 5.22 vs olap_off 3.67 → +1.55 months (p=4e-27, n_on=529). BRCA2-: olap_on 3.67 vs olap_off 3.74 → -0.07 months (p=0.019, n_on=4569). Olaparib is beneficial only in BRCA2 carriers; in non-carriers it is mildly detrimental.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive psa_ng_ml x mcrpc interaction on pfs_months — meaning the negative impact of higher PSA on PFS is attenuated in mCRPC patients (e.g., because PSA is less prognostic once disease is castration-resistant).
There is a meaningful ldh_u_l x mcrpc interaction on pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + albumin_g_dl + psa_ng_ml*mcrpc', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Strong positive interaction: +0.0153 month per ng/mL of PSA in mCRPC vs hormone-sensitive (p effectively 0). I.e., the per-unit harm of PSA on PFS is much less negative in mCRPC.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l*mcrpc', data=df).fit()

proposed_hypotheses.text: A parsimonious multivariable model containing age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, ldh_u_l, visceral_mets, the six treatments, and the brca2_mutation x treatment_olaparib interaction explains >95% of the variance in pfs_months in this cohort.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + mcrpc + psa_ng_ml + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + ldh_u_l + visceral_mets + treatment_enzalutamide + treatment_abiraterone + treatment_docetaxel + treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation + treatment_lu177_psma + treatment_pembrolizumab', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: R²=0.9518. All key prognostic features remain highly significant in expected directions: age +0.174, ecog -1.152, mcrpc -0.376, psa -0.0027, albumin +0.479, weight_loss -0.076, ldh -0.0005, visceral_mets -0.020. brca2_mutation main effect ≈0 (p=0.68), treatment_olaparib main effect ≈0 (p=0.72), but the brca2_mutation:treatment_olaparib interaction is +1.437 months (p effectively 0). The model confirms that the only treatment with a clinically meaningful PFS effect is olaparib, contingent on BRCA2 status.
